caseStudy.title: Serverless Data Pipeline
caseStudy.description: Event-driven ETL pipeline replacing a fragile batch system.
caseStudy.content: Full content of Case Study 3 goes here.
caseStudy.overview: A large retail client required a real-time data pipeline to ingest, transform, and store transactional data from thousands of point-of-sale terminals. The legacy batch processing system introduced delays of up to 6 hours, making inventory analytics nearly useless for same-day decisions.
caseStudy.problem: The nightly batch ETL jobs frequently failed silently, producing incomplete datasets that downstream analytics teams discovered only hours later. Data reconciliation was manual and time-consuming, and the monolithic pipeline had no partial-failure recovery.
caseStudy.solution: An event-driven serverless pipeline was built using managed streaming ingestion, stateless transformation functions, and a columnar data warehouse for analytics. Dead-letter queues and idempotent retry logic ensured no events were lost. Monitoring dashboards provided real-time visibility into pipeline health.

results.summary: Inventory teams now access near-real-time analytics, enabling same-day restocking decisions. The pipeline handles Black Friday-level traffic surges without configuration changes.
